title: Automated building energy modeling for energy retrofits using a large language model-based multi-agent framework
inputData: 混合数据（CAD图纸+设计文档+传感器数据）
contribution: 将建模时间减少90%以上(48分钟对比8-32小时)
abstract: This paper presents Data2BEM, a GPT-4-based multi-agent framework that automates end-to-end building energy modeling by parsing architectural drawings, specifications, and sensor data to generate and calibrate building energy simulations. Applied to a University of Cambridge office building, the system achieves ASHRAE Guideline 14-level calibration accuracy while reducing total modeling time by over 90% compared to professional manual practice, and demonstrates retrofit evaluation through gas boiler-to-heat pump electrification scenarios.
methodology: A multi-agent LLM framework (Data2BEM) with four specialized GPT-4-based agents (Information Retriever, Programmer, Result Analyzer, and Reviewer) that collaboratively decompose the building energy modeling workflow into 4 stages, 14 tasks, and 32 subtasks, parsing heterogeneous data sources (CAD drawings, design documents, sensor data) to generate, calibrate, and evaluate OpenStudio/EnergyPlus simulation models.
results: Data2BEM reduced total modeling time by over 90% (48 minutes versus 8-32 hours for human modelers) and achieved ASHRAE Guideline 14-level calibration accuracy with NMBE of 2.91%, CV-RMSE of 0.139, and R-squared of 0.972. The electrification retrofit scenario (replacing gas boilers with air-source heat pumps) reduced annual energy costs by 44% and carbon tax obligations approximately 5-fold.
